name: Cauliflower and Roquefort Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 large boiling potato, peeled and diced
A few drops of liquid pepper seasoning
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large, heavy Dutch oven over moderately high heat. Sauté onion for 1 minute, then cover and sweat for 10 minutes.
2. Halve and core the cauliflower. Reserve some florets and chop the remainder to total about 6 cups.
3. Peel and dice the potato. Add cauliflower and potato to the Dutch oven; cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
4. Add chicken broth, chives, Herbes de Provence, and liquid pepper seasoning. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer for 10 minutes, or until vegetables are just tender.
5. Cool slightly. Puree all but 1 1/2 cups of the mixture in batches using a food processor or blender, returning to pan.
6. In a small bowl, blend cream, egg yolks, and Armagnac. Add 1 cup of hot soup, beating constantly with a wire whisk.
7. Return to pan and add half of the crumbled Roquefort cheese. Heat very slowly, stirring constantly, until cheese melts and soup thickens slightly. Do not allow soup to boil.
8. Garnish soup with reserved cauliflower florets, crumbled Roquefort, and chives.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
